law: This sample shows one approach, which is to use a data-entry form with SwiftData that lets someone add, edit, and store data about animals.
Before SwiftData can store data from your app, the app must define the data model that represents the data.
As with , the definition applies the macro to ensure the class conforms to and to save the animal category data to a model container.
The design of allows the app to use the same view for both adding new animals and editing existing ones.

To ensure that the app’s interface remains approachable, the app limits how many earthquakes it displays based on: A date — To avoid overwhelming the map with too many markers, the app displays only one day’s worth of earthquakes at a time.
